MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2023 Vacated

Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. S. 425.16.; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-30-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIK SYVERSON, Esq. (Tel: 310-270-6000) for plaintiff;
Atty. ROHIT CHHABRA, Esq. of Chhabra Law Firm, PC (Tel: 650-564-7929) for defendant Alan Lee.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALAN LEE's Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. S. 425.16.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. S. 425.16.; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-30-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIK SYVERSON, Esq. (Tel: 310-270-6000) for plaintiff;
Atty. ROHIT CHHABRA, Esq. of Chhabra Law Firm, PC (Tel: 650-564-7929) for defendant Alan Lee.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALAN LEE's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.16.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Alan Lee's motion for attorney fees and costs regarding his CCP 425.16 motion to strike International Art Museum of America's ("IAMA") complaint is denied.
Plaintiff has voluntarily dismissed its complaint, but the "trial court may rule on the merits of the anti-SLAPP motion as it relates to a defendant's entitlement to fees where there is a pending request for fees." (Caitlin Insurance Co., Inc. v. Danko Meredith Law Firm, Inc. (2022) 73 Cal.App.5th 764, 775.)
The complaint pled that Lee defamed plaintiff with an online statement: "Here's the truth about this 'museum': It's actully [sic] a front for a massive money laundering scheme." (Cmplt. 2:3-8, Ex. A.)
"Section 425.16" sets "a two-step process for determining whether an action is a SLAPP. First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the challenged cause of action is one arising from protected activity If the court finds that such a showing has been made, it must then determine whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 88.) "Only a cause of action that satisfies both parts of the anti-SLAPP statute - i.e., that arises from protected speech or petitioning and lacks even minimal merit - is a SLAPP, subject to being stricken under the statute." (Tamkin v. CBS Broadcasting, Inc. (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 133, 142.)
The first CCP 425.16 step is undisputed. As to the second step, IAMA demonstrates that its complaint had at least "minimal merit." (Tamkin, 193 Cal.App.4th at 142.)
First, IAMA shows that Lee (alias Michael B.) was probably the defamatory statement's author. Using subpoenas and a party admission by Lee's counsel, IAMA showed that Lee's telephone number and Michael B.'s were identical, as was the number 689 in their e-addresses. (Opp. 2:13-3:6.) Lee argues that an "Unknown Publisher could have" schemed to frame him (Rply. 5:1-9), but that is improbable.
Second, the statement that IAMA is "a front for a massive money laundering scheme" is one of fact, not opinion. Lee's argument that the statement lacks "formality and polish" (Rply. 6:16-17) undersells the author's writing ability, which is considerable.
Third, IAMA demonstrates at least "minimal merit" as to the malice element of its claims. (Tamkin, 193 Cal.App.4th at 142.) It shows, e.g., that Lee did not contact it to investigate his money laundering statement. (Opp. 3:23-27.) Assuming that an actual malice standard applies, Lee repeats his improbable assertion that he did not make the defamatory statement - a statement he should have had serious doubts about. (See Rply. 7:15-17.)
No attorney fees are awarded to Lee as he is not the prevailing party. IAMA seeks attorney fees on the ground that Lee's motion was "frivolous." I disagree, so IAMA also is awarded no fees.
Lee's requests for judicial notice 2 and 3 are granted; the remainder are denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. S. 425.16.; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Continued To Mar-30-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ALAN LEE Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. S. 425.16

No appearance.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALAN LEE's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.16 is continued to March 30, 2023.

Plaintiff dismissed this action in its entirety on December 6, 2022. The court cannot rule on the anti-SLAPP motion given that dismissal. (See Catlin Ins. Co., Inc. v. Danko Meredith Law Firm, Inc. (2022) 73 Cal.App.5th 764, 774 ["[T]he trial court could not strike a complaint that [plaintiff] had already voluntarily dismissed."]; Law Offices of Andrew L. Ellis v. Yang (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 869, 879 [after a voluntary dismissal, "the trial court continues to have jurisdiction over the case for purposes of deciding if the plaintiffs are responsible for attorney fees and costs, but not to rule on the anti-SLAPP motion."].)

Plaintiff may calendar a motion to vacate the dismissal prior to the March 30, 2023, hearing. If plaintiff vacates the dismissal, the court will entertain the anti-SLAPP motion to strike and fee motion on March 30, 2023, based on the parties' filings on the register of actions.
If plaintiff does not vacate the dismissal by that time, the court will only entertain the fee motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT ALAN LEE's Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.16 is continued to March 30, 2023.

Plaintiff dismissed this action in its entirety on December 6, 2022. The court cannot rule on the anti-SLAPP motion given that dismissal. (See Catlin Ins. Co., Inc. v. Danko Meredith Law Firm, Inc. (2022) 73 Cal.App.5th 764, 774 ["[T]he trial court could not strike a complaint that [plaintiff] had already voluntarily dismissed."]; Law Offices of Andrew L. Ellis v. Yang (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 869, 879 [after a voluntary dismissal, "the trial court continues to have jurisdiction over the case for purposes of deciding if the plaintiffs are responsible for attorney fees and costs, but not to rule on the anti-SLAPP motion."].)
Plaintiff may calendar a motion to vacate the dismissal prior to the March 30, 2023, hearing. If plaintiff vacates the dismissal, the court will entertain the anti-SLAPP motion to strike and fee motion on March 30, 2023, based on the parties' filings on the register of actions. If plaintiff does not vacate the dismissal by that time, the court will only entertain the fee motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

Matter discussed.

The Court continues this matter to be heard on February 14, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.
